result: If I could change the world in just one way, I would make empathy as instinctive and immediate as breathing—a world where every person, without effort, fully feels the inner lives of others, not as an abstract concept but as vividly as their own. This wouldn't be a forced harmony, but a foundational shift where cruelty becomes literally unthinkable because the pain you cause another resonates in your own nerves, where understanding precedes judgment, and where loneliness evaporates because no one is ever truly unseen again. Every other fracture—greed, war, indifference—feeds on a deficit of felt connection. So I’d embed connection into the root of consciousness itself.

Every other fracture—greed, war, indifference—feeds on a deficit of felt connection. So I’d embed connection into the root of consciousness itself.
